# Code of conduct

sui-id follows a short, plain-language code of conduct.

## In one paragraph

Treat people working on sui-id, asking questions about it, or reporting
problems with it the way you would want to be treated. Argue with ideas, not
with the people who hold them. Assume the other person is acting in good
faith until you have a clear reason to think otherwise. If a discussion
becomes personal, step away.

## Specifically

- **Welcome.** Newcomers, beginners, and people who have not contributed
  before are part of the project's audience, not an inconvenience to it.
- **Disagree carefully.** It is fine, sometimes necessary, to say "I think
  this is wrong, here is why." It is not fine to belittle, mock, or attack
  the person making the proposal.
- **No harassment.** This includes the obvious — slurs, threats, sexualized
  attention, doxxing — and the less obvious: piling on, brigading from
  external venues, or repeatedly raising the same complaint after it has
  been answered.
- **Take feedback gracefully.** Reviewers are spending their time on your
  contribution. Even when they get something wrong, the right response is
  "I see your point, but here's what I meant" — not escalation.
- **Step back when needed.** If a thread has gone sideways, the maintainers
  may close or lock it. That is not personal; it is hygiene.

## Reporting

If something feels wrong, contact the maintainers privately. We take reports
seriously, we keep them confidential where the law and the situation allow,
and we will tell you what we did about them.

## Consequences

The maintainers may, at their discretion, edit or remove comments, close
issues or pull requests, or temporarily or permanently restrict participation
in the project for people whose conduct violates this document.

## Scope

This document applies to all project venues — the repository, issue tracker,
pull requests, discussions, security advisory threads, and any official chat
channel — and to behaviour outside those venues that has a clear effect on
the project or its participants.
